items.headline: Israel strikes Lebanon killing over 200 as ceasefire scope dispute escalates
items.body: Israeli bombings across Lebanon killed at least 203 people in deadliest attacks since March, with Netanyahu saying Lebanon excluded from Iran ceasefire. Iran warns Washington must choose between ceasefire or continued proxy war through Israel.

items.headline: Russia declares Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine for two days
items.body: Putin ordered cessation of combat operations from April 11 through April 12 for Orthodox Easter after Zelenskyy signaled openness. Ukraine had urged reimposition of Russian oil sanctions after temporary US waiver allowed Moscow billions in extra profit.

items.headline: Venezuela passes mining reform law to attract foreign investment after decades
items.body: National Assembly unanimously approved bill replacing framework from 1990s with flexible tax regime and legal guarantees for international investors. Reform pushed by Acting President Rodríguez targets US companies in industry long exploited by criminal groups.

items.headline: Artemis II crew returns from Moon in first crewed lunar mission since 1972
items.body: Orion capsule splashed down in Pacific completing mission that sent first person of color and first woman around the Moon. Landing marks halfway point in NASA program to establish permanent lunar presence by decade end.

no.headline: Government commission recommends Norway not pursue nuclear power until at least the 2040s
no.body: Report delivered April 8 concludes nuclear is not economically viable given abundant hydropower and high regulatory costs. Decision ends first comprehensive review since the 1970s and could delay private proposals for small modular reactors.
